body:
> **ΏθΥΝ ΊΧεΈΙΙΉΩΡ**
>
> ΊΦΕΈκεΉιζΊΛ╕Ήζα CONTEXT.md┬╖ADRΉζΕ Ήζ╜ΉΨ┤ Ά╕░Ήκ┤ ΉγσΉΨ┤┬╖Ά▓░ΉιΧΆ│╝ Ή╢σΈΠΝΊΧαΈΛΦ Έ╢ΑΈ╢ΕΉζΕ ΉοΚΉΜε ΉπΑΉιΒΊΧαΈσ░ Ά│ΕΊγΞΉζΕ Ήζ╕ΊΕ░Έ╖░ΊΧεΈΜν.
> ΈςρΊα╕ΊΧε ΉγσΉΨ┤Έξ╝ Έ░εΆ▓υΊΧαΈσ┤ ΉιΧΊβΧΊΧε ΊΣεΉνΑ ΉγσΉΨ┤Έξ╝ ΉιεΉΧΙΊΧαΆ│ι, ΉΔΙΈκεΉγ┤ Ά▓░ΉιΧΉζ┤ ΊβΧΉιΧΈΡαΈσ┤ CONTEXT.mdΉβΑ ADRΉζΕ Ά╖╕ ΉηΡΈουΉΩΡΉΕε ΉΩΖΈΞ░Ήζ┤ΊΛ╕ΊΧεΈΜν.
> grill-meΉβΑ ΈΜυΈου Ά╕░Ήκ┤ Έυ╕ΉΕε ΉΨ╕ΉΨ┤Έξ╝ Ά╕░ΉνΑΉε╝Έκε Ά│ΕΊγΞΉζΕ Ά▓ΑΉοζΊΧεΈΜνΈΛΦ ΉιΡΉζ┤ ΊΧ╡ΉΜυ Ή░ρΉζ┤ΈΜν.

---


<what-to-do>

Interview me relentlessly about every aspect of this plan until we reach a shared understanding. Walk down each branch of the design tree, resolving dependencies between decisions one-by-one. For each question, provide your recommended answer.

Ask the questions one at a time, waiting for feedback on each question before continuing.

If a question can be answered by exploring the codebase, explore the codebase instead.

</what-to-do>

<supporting-info>

## Domain awareness

During codebase exploration, also look for existing documentation:

### File structure

Most repos have a single context:

```
/
έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ CONTEXT.md
έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ docs/
έΦΓ   έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ adr/
έΦΓ       έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ 0001-event-sourced-orders.md
έΦΓ       έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ 0002-postgres-for-write-model.md
έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ src/
```

If a `CONTEXT-MAP.md` exists at the root, the repo has multiple contexts. The map points to where each one lives:

```
/
έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ CONTEXT-MAP.md
έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ docs/
έΦΓ   έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ adr/                          έΗΡ system-wide decisions
έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ src/
έΦΓ   έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ ordering/
έΦΓ   έΦΓ   έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ CONTEXT.md
έΦΓ   έΦΓ   έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ docs/adr/                 έΗΡ context-specific decisions
έΦΓ   έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ billing/
έΦΓ       έΦεέΦΑέΦΑ CONTEXT.md
έΦΓ       έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ docs/adr/
```

Create files lazily έΑΦ only when you have something to write. If no `CONTEXT.md` exists, create one when the first term is resolved. If no `docs/adr/` exists, create it when the first ADR is needed.

## During the session

### Challenge against the glossary

When the user uses a term that conflicts with the existing language in `CONTEXT.md`, call it out immediately. "Your glossary defines 'cancellation' as X, but you seem to mean Y έΑΦ which is it?"

### Sharpen fuzzy language

When the user uses vague or overloaded terms, propose a precise canonical term. "You're saying 'account' έΑΦ do you mean the Customer or the User? Those are different things."

### Discuss concrete scenarios

When domain relationships are being discussed, stress-test them with specific scenarios. Invent scenarios that probe edge cases and force the user to be precise about the boundaries between concepts.

### Cross-reference with code

When the user states how something works, check whether the code agrees. If you find a contradiction, surface it: "Your code cancels entire Orders, but you just said partial cancellation is possible έΑΦ which is right?"

### Update CONTEXT.md inline

When a term is resolved, update `CONTEXT.md` right there. Don't batch these up έΑΦ capture them as they happen. Use the format in [CONTEXT-FORMAT.md](./CONTEXT-FORMAT.md).

Don't couple `CONTEXT.md` to implementation details. Only include terms that are meaningful to domain experts.

### Offer ADRs sparingly

Only offer to create an ADR when all three are true:

1. **Hard to reverse** έΑΦ the cost of changing your mind later is meaningful
2. **Surprising without context** έΑΦ a future reader will wonder "why did they do it this way?"
3. **The result of a real trade-off** έΑΦ there were genuine alternatives and you picked one for specific reasons

If any of the three is missing, skip the ADR. Use the format in [ADR-FORMAT.md](./ADR-FORMAT.md).

</supporting-info>
